questions.question: The ideal stoichiometric ratio of air to fuel of each cylinder in your engine is 14:1. This ratio is metered by your Avstar fuel injector in direct relation to the weight of airflow entering the throttle body, equally divided by the fuel distributor. If the total volume of air entering the throttle body is 455 pounds, how many gallons are currently being consumed by each of your four cylinders?

At 7 NM, a brand-new Allegiant Airlines Boeing 737-8 MAX is beginning the descent and will maintain a final approach ground speed of 148 knots. At 3 NM, a Piper Seneca maintains a ground speed of 110 knots. How far out on final will the Boeing be when the Seneca crosses the threshold?
In the previous question, how much slower would the MAX have to fly to increase the separation by 1 NM?
questions.context: Referenced question 2: At 7 NM, a brand-new Allegiant Airlines Boeing 737-8 MAX is beginning the descent and will maintain a final approach ground speed of 148 knots. At 3 NM, a Piper Seneca maintains a ground speed of 110 knots. How far out on final will the Boeing be when the Seneca crosses the threshold?

questions.question: You are flying the RNAV approach into San Diego, CA which requires a descent rate of 3.5°. The tower reports an 18-kt headwind and your groundspeed is 125 KTS. What is the required rate of descent to maintain the glide path?

A search-and-rescue mission is dispatched to the site of a possible hiker stuck in the desert, 82 SM away on a course of 200°. The current wind is 130° at 45 knots. The airplane, a C.A.P. Cessna 182T, contains 60 gallons of fuel, burns 15 GPH, and flies at 142 KTAS. How long can the C182 circle the desert before returning if they want to land with 30 minutes of fuel remaining?

On a cross-country flight, you tune a nearby VOR. While flying a straight line, you cross the 215° radial at 10:12:20. You then cross the 209° radial at 10:14:50. How far are you from the VOR if your groundspeed is 436 km/hr?

After flying 72 NM of your planned 150 NM flight, you check your position and find you are 4° right of course. If you wish to immediately turn direct to your destination, what heading correction should you apply?

While performing power-off stalls, a blockage in the induction system results in failure of your engine, at 5,500 feet. The elevation of the nearest airport due west, 6.5 NM away, is 826 feet. The average winds aloft from the surface to your altitude is 200° at 23 knots. If the POH dictates a calm-wind glide ratio of 11.57:1, and assuming each knot of headwind decreases your range by 0.2 NM, will you make the airport, and by how many miles?

choices.text: You will make the airport with .8 NM to spare
You will make the airport with 2.4 NM to spare

questions.correctChoice: You will make the airport with .8 NM to spare

questions.question: You recall that in the "Gimli Glider" incident, the Boeing 767 achieved a glide ratio of 12.15:1. If you were instead in the same 767 with the same conditions as the accident, how much further would you be able to stretch your glide?
questions.context: Referenced question 8: While performing power-off stalls, a blockage in the induction system results in failure of your engine, at 5,500 feet. questions.question: Climbing out of an airport in Arizona (Elev. 2000') you are maintaining 110 KIAS and need to achieve 510'/NM. In calm winds, what is the required climb rate? (Outside air temperature is 90°F and altimeter setting is 30.04"Hg).
After a flight, a pilot sees they just reached 3,000 hours total time in their logbook. How many weeks of time airborne is that equivalent to?

Approximately 8.2 NM from the departure end of the runway, there is a ski resort perched atop a 4,000-foot mountain. Using the following conditions, what will your required climb performance be upon reaching the departure end at 50' to clear the resort by 1,000 feet? [[TABLE]]

The jet stream at FL310 (ISA-15°C) is 120° at 105 knots, whereas at FL270 (ISA-11°C) it is 160° at 143 knots. On a true course of 210° and 450 KIAS, what is the difference in time enroute if your destination is 1,817 KM away?
The Goodyear blimp contains nearly 300,000 cubic feet of helium. How many cubic meters does that equate to?
Helium weighs 5.171 grams per cubic foot. How many pounds of helium are inside the Goodyear blimp?
questions.context: Referenced question 20: The Goodyear blimp contains nearly 300,000 cubic feet of helium. How many cubic meters does that equate to?

questions.question: You spend $404.67 Canadian Dollars to fill your aircraft with 200 liters of gas. If the conversion rate is 1.39 CAN:1 USD, what is the fuel price in USD per gallon?

An aerobatic warbird weighs 5,400 pounds with a CG of 142.2". During its routine it burns 12 gallons of smoke oil (8lbs/gal) at a CG of 10". Additionally, it burns 35 GPH of 100LL at a CG of 148". The routine is 12 minutes long. What is the landing CG?
questions.answerKeyType: CG Change Used 6.7 pounds per gallon 100LL

questions.question: What is the distance in nautical miles between the following two coordinates? [[NEXT_LINE]]A = N44° 44' 46.98'' W93° 5' 43.10'' [[NEXT_LINE]]B = N44° 52' 57.09'' W93° 12' 42.54''
Reference the previous question. What is the true course from point A to point B?
questions.context: Referenced question 24: What is the distance in nautical miles between the following two coordinates? [[NEXT_LINE]]A = N44° 44' 46.98'' W93° 5' 43.10'' [[NEXT_LINE]]B = N44° 52' 57.09'' W93° 12' 42.54''

questions.question: You are flying from Airport A to Airport B, a distance of 720 NM, on a course of 090°. The True Airspeed is 160 knots. Winds aloft are 090° at 40 knots. Fuel burn is 12 GPH and usable fuel is 72 gallons. Your filed alternate is 100 NM beyond airport B on the same course. At the ETP, do you have enough fuel to continue to Airport B and then proceed to the alternate with a required 45-minute reserve?

choices.text: Yes, with more than your required reserve
Yes, but landing at your alternate with less than required reserve
No, you can not reach airport B
No, you can reach airport B but not the alternate with reserve

questions.correctChoice: No, you can reach airport B but not the alternate with reserve

questions.question: What distance from airport B would the fuel required be the same to continue to airport B or to return back to airport A?
questions.context: Referenced question 26: You are flying from Airport A to Airport B, a distance of 720 NM, on a course of 090°. The True Airspeed is 160 knots. Winds aloft are 090° at 40 knots. questions.question: Your two 34-gallon fuel tanks were filled with water (8.35 LBS/GAL) instead of jet-A (6.7 LBS/GAL). How much more would the aircraft weigh?

You've taken off in a King Air 200 with a takeoff weight of 12,500 LBS and abruptly hit a patch of moderate turbulence. The two passengers in the aft-facing seats (214") fall into the next row of seats (253"). If the C.G. on takeoff was 192.3", what is it now if each passenger weighed 200 LBS?
Distractions in the cabin have left you 4 NM off course after flying for 30 NM. How many degrees would you need to turn to parallel your original course?

Reference the previous question. If you have 68 NM remaining in your flight, how many additional degrees would you need to turn if you wanted to intercept your original course 20 NM before arriving at your destination?
questions.context: Referenced question 30: Distractions in the cabin have left you 4 NM off course after flying for 30 NM. How many degrees would you need to turn to parallel your original course?

questions.question: You are flying on a magnetic course of 220° with an airspeed of 510 KTAS. The preflight weather briefing shows textual winds aloft forecast at your altitude of 34,000' are 651553. The magnetic variation in the area is 15°E. If that forecast is true, what is your magnetic heading?
Referring to the previous question, what is your effective true airspeed?
questions.context: Referenced question 32: You are flying on a magnetic course of 220° with an airspeed of 510 KTAS. The preflight weather briefing shows textual winds aloft forecast at your altitude of 34,000' are 651553. The magnetic variation in the area is 15°E. If that forecast is true, what is your magnetic heading?

questions.question: The distance from the hangar to your airplane is 650 meters. How many miles do you walk if you make 8 round trips in the day?
Reference the previous question. If you are sitting in the hangar studying and are called to get out to the airplane right away, how fast do you need to run to be there in 2 minutes?
questions.context: Referenced question 34: The distance from the hangar to your airplane is 650 meters. How many miles do you walk if you make 8 round trips in the day?

questions.question: When you start your engine in KSAT, you have 18.7 quarts of oil. Upon entering the pattern in KMSP, you are indicating 9.3 quarts. If the flight is 167 minutes long, what is the rate that you've been burning oil?
ATC tells you to enter a five-mile final and to slow to your final approach speed. At 5 nm you are at 56 mph, the wind is currently a direct headwind at 39 knots. How long do you spend on final?
Convert 350 cm into yards.
Convert 2,280 feet per minute into statute miles per hour.

You are attempting to build an experimental amphibious aircraft with two cylindrical pontoons as floats. You want the total buoyancy to exceed the maximum structural weight by 75%. For every cubic foot of pontoon, 62.4lbs of buoyant force is created. The pontoon dimensions are 18" in diameter and 20 ft long. What is the maximum structural weight?
